title: Sith warbird
content:

{{Quote|Mainstays among the Massassi infantry even today, the warbirds are used as mounted assault avians that can carry a rider into battle and rip apart enemy soldiers with their beaks.|Sorzus Syn|<ref name="BoS" />}}
The '''Sith warbird''' was a [[Species/Legends|species]] of flightless [[Bird/Legends|bird]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Khar Delba]] that was commonly used as an [[Artillery/Legends|artillery]] mount by the [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]], a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] species from the world [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]. By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Discovery of Korriban|arrival]] of [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Exiles]] on the planet Korriban around [[6900 BBY]], warbirds were a mainstay of the [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] [[Infantry/Legends|infantry]] of the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]], and the birds were later used by the [[Sith Empire (Exar Kun)/Legends|Sith Empire]] of the fallen [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] warbirds were notorious for their bad temper, and they typically used their large size to ram and trample down their prey.

==Biology and appearance==
[[File:SithWarbird-JATM.jpg|thumb|left|200px|A warbird being approached by a humanoid being]]
[[Sith/Legends|Sith]] warbirds were large, flightless [[Bird/Legends|birds]]<ref name="TSC" /> from the [[Water/Legends|icy]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Khar Delba]]. When fully grown,<ref name="CSWE" /> they stood at approximately six [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in height from ground to top of the head, and ten meters long from head to tail.<ref name="TSC" /> In place of wings, warbirds had two<ref name="UA" /> short<ref name="JATM" /> dual-clawed [[Limb/Legends|arms]], and the birds also sported two legs and a large,<ref name="UA" /> blunt [[Mouth/Legends|beak]], which was used for devouring prey. Their feathers ranged from [[Color/Legends|yellow]]<ref name="JATM" /> to yellowish-green in color, and they had two [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], with iris colors ranging from black<ref name="UA" /> to blue.<ref name="BoS" /> The creatures also had extremely thick hides, which allowed hooks and spikes to be embedded into the sides of domesticated warbirds without causing the creatures to come to harm.<ref name="JATM" />

==Behavior==
Sith warbirds were predators and were notorious for their bad tempered nature, attacking anything that they saw as a threat. Though lacking in power, warbirds could move with great speed and maneuverability; they typically used their large size to ram and trample their prey, and they were also known to use their large beaks to gore their enemies.<ref name="JATM" />

==History==
{{Quote|During the same war that birthed the silooth, the Sith introduced the warbird and the war behemoth.|Sorzus Syn|<ref name="BoS" />}}
Warbirds originated on the planet Khar Delba<ref name="CSWE" /> in the [[Stygian Caldera]],<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> where they inhabited the world's frozen [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]].<ref name="CSWE" /> Khar Delba lay within the bounds of the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]],<ref name="TEA" /> a civilization established by the [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]] [[Species/Legends|species]], and, during the early history of the Empire, the birds were discovered and subsequently domesticated by the Sith.<ref name="BoS" />

In the midst of an [[Unidentified war (Sith Empire)|ancient conflict]] in which the Empire became embroiled, the domesticated warbirds were pressed into [[Ground warfare/Legends|military service]] to be used as [[Artillery/Legends|artillery]] mounts,<ref name="BoS" /> as was common practice for tamed animals within the Empire at the [[Time/Legends|time]].<ref name="TSC" /> By the time of the [[Discovery of Korriban|arrival]] of [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Exiles]] on Korriban<ref name="BoS" /> around [[6900 BBY]] following their [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|banishment]] from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> warbird mounts were mainstays of the [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] [[Infantry/Legends|infantry]] forces employed by the Sith, and the Dark [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Sith alchemist/Legends|alchemist]] [[Sorzus Syn]] mentioned the creatures in a [[Sorzus Syn's chronicle|chronicle]] of her encounters with the Sith.<ref name="BoS" />

The first Sith Empire ultimately [[Second battle of Korriban|collapsed]] in [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]],<ref name="NEC" /> and warbirds were later used by the [[Sith Empire (Exar Kun)/Legends|Sith Empire]] of the fallen Jedi [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]<ref name="JATM" /> in [[3996 BBY]].<ref name="NEC" />

==Sith warbirds in the galaxy==
[[File:SithWarbird.jpg|thumb|right|200px|A Sith warbird wearing a battle harness]]
Sith warbirds saw common usage among the military forces of the Sith Empire as battle mounts,<ref name="BoS" /> and when employed for combat purposes, the birds were fitted to carry [[battle harness]]es, which contained a [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] to house the rider and a mounted [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannon]] supported by a [[Fusion generator/Legends|fusion generator]].<ref name="TSC" /> The Sith used the birds as mobile platforms that were deployed in front of infantry columns to weaken enemy ground-based [[Troop/Legends|troops]]. During the Great Sith War, the creatures were also commonly used for [[Scout/Legends|scouting]] and reconnaissance assignments because, as living creatures, they were less likely to be spotted by enemy detection devices.<ref name="JATM" />

After the end of the Great Sith War, Sith warbirds continued to be found throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], with survivors of the conflict [[Reproduction/Legends|breeding]] on worlds where battles had been fought by the Sith. These wild-once-more warbirds were prone to terrorizing local forms of wildlife that posed little threat to them.<ref name="JATM" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Sith warbirds were created by [[Michael Mikaelian]] and were first mentioned in ''[[The Sith Compendium]]'', an article that appeared in the [[2001]] [[Wizards of the Coast]] magazine [[Star Wars Gamer 5|''Star Wars Gamer'' 5]].<ref name="TSC" /> The warbirds later received a further mention in the [[2004]] [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game|roleplaying]] sourcebook ''[[Ultimate Adversaries]]'', which became the first source to provide a visual depiction of the creatures, in an [[:File:SithWarbird.jpg|image]] by the artist [[Matt Hatton]].<ref name="UA" />
